title: Add coupon discount support to charge endpoint
summary: This PR introduces coupon-discount support but has several blocking issues: a console.log that dumps the full charge payload (card data, PII) to logs in violation of PCI DSS; a logic bug that always produces a zero discount_amount; and a FixedCouponStrategy that can produce a negative charge amount. Additionally, exceptions from the discount step are silently swallowed, couponCode is extracted from the raw body before schema validation, and the new code has widespread naming/style problems throughout.

findings.title: Full charge body (card number, CVV, PII) logged to console
findings.message: `console.log('DEBUG charge body:', JSON.stringify(theData))` serialises and logs the raw request body, which contains card number, expiry, CVV, and customer PII. This is a PCI DSS violation and must be removed before merge. If request logging is needed, use a structured logger that masks/redacts sensitive fields.

findings.title: discount_amount is always 0 — payload.amount is overwritten before the subtraction
findings.message: Line 24 sets `payload.amount = finalAmount` (the post-discount value). Line 36 then computes `payload.amount - finalAmount`, which is `finalAmount - finalAmount = 0`. The client will always receive `discount_amount: 0`. Fix: store the original amount before applying the discount, e.g. `const originalAmount = payload.amount;`, then compute `response.discount_amount = originalAmount - finalAmount`.

findings.title: FixedCouponStrategy can produce a negative charge amount
findings.message: `return amount - this.amt` has no lower-bound guard. A $3 charge (300 cents) with FIVEOFF (500 cents) produces -200. A negative amount sent to the payment gateway will either error unpredictably or, worse, credit the card. Fix: `return Math.max(0, amount - this.amt)` and decide the business rule for over-discounting (reject the coupon, charge $0, etc.).

findings.title: Discount errors are silently swallowed — charge proceeds at full price without notification
findings.message: The inner `try/catch` catches any exception from `doStuff` and does nothing (`// TODO`). If the coupon lookup throws for any reason the customer is silently charged full price even though they supplied a coupon. Either propagate the error to the caller or—if silent fallback is the intended policy—log it at WARN level and document the decision.

findings.title: couponCode is read from raw body before schema validation
findings.message: `couponCode` is extracted from the un-validated `req.body` before `chargeSchema.parse()` runs. If `chargeSchema` does not declare `couponCode`, the field is never validated (type, length, character set) and any attacker-controlled string flows directly into `CouponHandlerFactory.build`. Either add `couponCode` to `chargeSchema` and read it from `payload`, or at minimum read it after `parse()`.

findings.title: Transaction object is mutated and typed as `any`, discarding type safety
findings.message: `const response: any = transaction` loses all type information for the transaction. The fields `discount_amount` and `coupon_code` are grafted onto the object at runtime. Define a typed response interface (e.g. `ChargeResponse`) that includes these optional fields, construct a new object rather than mutating the domain object, and remove the `any` cast.

findings.title: coupon_code is always included in the response, even when no coupon was provided
findings.message: When `couponCode` is `undefined` (no coupon supplied), the response still contains `coupon_code: undefined` (serialised as absent or null depending on the serialiser) and `discount_amount: 0`, which is confusing to clients. Omit these fields from the response when no coupon was applied.

findings.title: Function named `doStuff` in a payments service
findings.message: `doStuff` is not an acceptable name for a production payments function. Rename to something descriptive, e.g. `applyCouponDiscount(amount, couponCode)`. Similarly, local variables `tmp` and `result2` should be renamed to `strategy` and `discountedAmount` respectively.

findings.title: `var` used instead of `const` in TypeScript
findings.message: `var x = ...` (line 16) and `var tmp = ...` (line 58) use `var`, which has function scope and no block-level safety. In a TypeScript codebase these should be `const`. Also, the private field `p` on `PercentageCouponStrategy` and `amt` on `FixedCouponStrategy` should use descriptive names (`percentage`/`rate` and `discountAmount`).

findings.title: Loose equality `==` used for string comparisons; long if-else chain instead of a map
findings.message: All comparisons in `CouponHandlerFactory.build` use `==` instead of `===`. In TypeScript, always prefer strict equality. Additionally, the long if-else chain is hard to maintain; replace it with a plain lookup map: `const COUPONS: Record<string, AbstractCouponStrategy> = { WELCOME10: new PercentageCouponStrategy(0.1), … }` and return `COUPONS[code] ?? null`.

findings.title: Debug console.log left in production code path
findings.message: `console.log('DEBUG: unknown coupon code:', couponCode)` logs unvalidated user input to stdout. Use the application's structured logger at the appropriate level (e.g. `logger.warn`) and remove the `DEBUG:` prefix. Raw `console.log` calls should not exist in service-layer code.

findings.title: Abstract class adds no value over a TypeScript interface here
findings.message: `AbstractCouponStrategy` has a single abstract method and no shared state or concrete behaviour. A TypeScript interface (`interface CouponStrategy { apply(amount: number): number }`) is idiomatic and lighter. The abstract class pattern is worth the overhead only when shared implementation is needed.

findings.title: No unit tests for coupon math — explicitly flagged TODO in the PR
findings.message: The PR description marks unit tests for the coupon calculations as a TODO follow-up. Given that this code handles money arithmetic (rounding, fixed discounts, negative-amount edge case), tests must exist before this merges. At minimum: percent discount rounding, fixed discount at-boundary (amount == discount, amount < discount), and unknown code passthrough.

findings.title: Coupon codes are hardcoded — requires a deploy to add, change, or expire a coupon
findings.message: All supported coupon codes are baked into the source. Adding a new coupon or expiring an old one requires a code change and a full deploy. Consider moving coupons to a config file, environment variable, or database table so they can be managed without a redeploy. At minimum, a code-level comment explaining this limitation would set the right expectations.

findings.title: Commented-out dead code should be removed
findings.message: The `FREESHIP` block is commented out and adds noise. Remove it; version control preserves history if it is ever needed again.
